data.title: Jefferies’ Wood Ditches Bitcoin, Warning Quantum Computing Could Break It
data.description:
Jefferies' Christopher Wood eliminated a 10% Bitcoin allocation in favor of gold, warning that quantum computing advances could undermine cryptographic security as researchers estimate 30% of circulating supply remains vulnerable to future attacks.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/jefferies-wood-ditches-bitcoin-warning-quantum-computing-could-break-it/

data.title: Address Poisoning Attack Results in Significant USDT Loss
data.description: According to Foresight News, Cyvers Alerts has reported an address poisoning attack resulting in the loss of 509,000 USDT. The victim intended to transfer funds to the address 0xe842....D3E6F but mistakenly sent them to the attacker's address 0xe842....f3e6F. Previously, the victim had already transferred 5,000 USDT to the attacker's address.

data.title: Meme Activity Surges to 50% of Solana DEX Weekly Trading Volume
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, data from Blockworks Research indicates that meme activity has returned to account for 50% of the weekly trading volume on Solana's decentralized exchanges (DEX) after a six-month hiatus. Notably, the proportion of meme activity in Solana DEX trading volume has seen positive growth for three consecutive weeks.

data.title: Galaxy Research Director: Stablecoin yield provisions become a "deadlock" in crypto structure legislation; Senate hearings to resume as early as late January.
data.description: Galaxy Research Director Alex Thorn stated in an article on the X platform that although Senate Banking Committee Chairman Tim Scott has not yet announced a new hearing date, the earliest the committee could reconvene for the revision hearings is the week of January 26-30, due to the Senate recess next week. The Senate Agriculture Committee, which handles CFTC-related matters, had previously postponed its revision hearings to January 27. Tim Scott had previously announced a postponement of the Crypto Markets Structure Act hearings. It is understood that stablecoin yields are a key sticking point in the negotiations. Bank lobbying groups are actively pushing for restrictions on stablecoin rewards, fearing that interest-bearing stablecoins could siphon off bank deposits and destabilize the banking system. Compromises proposed to gain congressional support were ultimately deemed unacceptable by the stablecoin industry, with some believing the issue is a matter of survival. Other unresolved issues include restrictions on DeFi and illicit activities, as well as restrictions on tokenized security innovation.

data.title: RollX, the Perp DEX protocol within the Base ecosystem, has launched the ROLL token, with a total airdrop of 180 million tokens.
data.description: According to the official announcement, RollX, the Perp DEX protocol based on the Base network, officially launched its token roll and genesis airdrop on January 16th at 19:00, with a total supply of 180 million tokens. The airdrop is open to Trade & LP points users and addresses participating in activities such as Galxe and Binance Wallet. 25% will be unlocked at TGE, and the remaining 75% will be released linearly over 6 months. Galxe and Lightning rewards are 100% available immediately. The deadline for claiming the first batch of airdrops is January 26th at 19:00. The official announcement also stated that a more competitive V2 incentive program will be launched after TGE.

data.title: Cryptocurrency stocks opened mixed, with Riot rising 6.7%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the opening of the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 54 points, the S&P 500 rose 0.18%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.4%. Cryptocurrency stocks showed mixed performance: ETHZilla fell 0.37%, Circle fell 0.39%, Coinbase fell 0.56%, Tron rose 1.05%, and Riot rose 6.7%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Riot Platforms Shares Rise Following AMD Data Center Lease Agreement
data.description: According to Foresight News, Riot Platforms (RIOT) experienced a 7.7% increase in its stock price. This rise follows the company's announcement of a data center lease agreement with AMD.

data.title: Significant BTC and ETH Options Expiry with Notable Put Call Ratios
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on January 16, Greeks.live macro researcher Adam released options data indicating the expiration of 20,000 BTC options. The Put Call Ratio for these options stands at 1.39, with a maximum pain point of $92,000 and a nominal value of $2.3 billion. Additionally, 120,000 ETH options are set to expire, featuring a Put Call Ratio of 1.04, a maximum pain point of $3,200, and a nominal value of $430 million.

data.title: Nasdaq issues violation notice to Bitcoin mining company Canaan Technology
data.description: Canaan Creative, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company, disclosed that it received a written notice from Nasdaq on January 14, stating that the company violated Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(2) because the closing price of its American Depositary Shares (ADSs) was below US$1.00 per share for 30 consecutive trading days. According to the notice, Canaan Creative must raise the closing price of its ADSs to above US$1 by July 13, 2026, and maintain this level for 10 consecutive trading days to avoid delisting. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Today, US Bitcoin ETFs saw a net inflow of 1,474 BTC, and Ethereum ETFs saw a net inflow of 68,853 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, today the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 1,474 BTC, with a 7-day net inflow of 15,511 BTC; the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 68,853 ETH, with a 7-day net inflow of 93,878 ETH; and the Solana ETF saw a net inflow of 36,579 SOL, with a 7-day net inflow of 348,938 SOL.

data.title: ETH Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass indicates that if ETH surpasses $3,470, the cumulative short liquidation intensity on major centralized exchanges could reach $1.3 billion. Conversely, if ETH falls below $3,148, the cumulative long liquidation intensity on these exchanges could amount to $1.137 billion.

data.title: Riot Platforms Sells Bitcoin to Fund Data Center Development
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company Riot Platforms has announced the sale of 1,080 Bitcoins, generating approximately $96 million. The proceeds will be used to purchase land in Rockdale for a data center project. Additionally, Riot Platforms has entered into a data center lease and service agreement with Advanced Micro Devices (AMD). This agreement will deploy 25 megawatts of critical IT load capacity at the Rockdale campus, with the initial phase starting in January 2026 and completion expected by May 2026.

data.title: BRICS Nation Russia Drafts Bill To Make Crypto Assets ‘Commonplace’ in Daily Life: Report
data.description:
Digital assets are poised to become a routine part of daily life in Russia as lawmakers advance legislation to remove them from special financial regulation, according a top Russian financial official.
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2026/01/16/brics-nation-russia-drafts-bill-to-make-crypto-assets-commonplace-in-daily-life-report/
